content_brief.title: Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? Here's How to Get It Back Fast
content_brief.meta_description: Is your landlord withholding your security deposit? Learn your tenant rights, state deadlines, legal deductions, and how to send a demand letter in 60 seconds.
content_outline: Introduction: The Frustration of Unreturned Security Deposits
What Landlords Can and Cannot Deduct From Your Deposit
5 Steps to Take When Your Landlord Won't Return Your Deposit
Why a Demand Letter Works Better Than Texting or Calling
How to Generate a Legal Demand Letter in 60 Seconds (No Lawyer Needed)
Conclusion: Stop Waiting and Start Demanding What's Yours
key_points: Landlords may only deduct for damages beyond normal wear and tear, unpaid rent, or specific lease violations depending on state law
Most states require landlords to return security deposits within 14 to 60 days; missing deadlines can entitle tenants to statutory penalties or treble damages
Sending a formal letter of demand is the fastest, most cost-effective way to force compliance before escalating to small claims court
Deposits-Back.com enables tenants to generate and send a professional, state-compliant security deposit demand letter in 60 seconds for $19.99 with no sign-up or subscription
content_brief.cta: Stop letting your landlord hold your money hostage. Visit Deposits-Back.com right now and send a professional security deposit demand letter in just 60 seconds for only $19.99. No sign-up. No subscription. No stress.
content_brief.target_audience: Frustrated tenants and renters across the United States who are dealing with landlords wrongfully withholding, delaying, or refusing to return security deposits
